# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_bd_subnet (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `bd` (String) The name of the Bridge Domain.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description for the Subnet.
- `enable_vip` (Boolean) Determines if the Subnet should be treated as a VIP; used when the BD is extended to multiple sites. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `gateway` (String) The IPv4 or IPv6 gateway address for the Subnet.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ip_data_plane_learning` (String) Whether IP data plane learning is enabled or disabled. The APIC defaults to `enabled` when unset during creation.
- `mask` (Number) The subnet mask for the Subnet. This is the number associated with CIDR notation. For IPv4 addresses, accepted values range between `0` and `32`. For IPv6 addresses, accepted Values range between `0` and `128`.
- `name_alias` (String) The alias for the current object. This relates to the nameAlias field in ACI.
- `nd_prefix_policy` (String) The IPv6 Neighbor Discovery Prefix Policy to associate with the Subnet.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `preferred` (Boolean) Determines if the Subnet is preferred over all available Subnets. Only one Subnet per Address Family (IPv4/IPv6). can be preferred in the Bridge Domain. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `route_profile` (String) The Route Profile to the associate with the Subnet.
- `route_profile_l3_out` (String) The L3 Out that contains the associated Route Profile.
- `scope` (String) Determines the scope of the Subnet. The `private` option only allows communication with hosts in the same VRF. The `public` option allows the Subnet to be advertised outside of the ACI Fabric, and allows communication with hosts in other VRFs. The shared option limits communication to hosts in either the same VRF or the shared VRF. The value is a list of options, `private` and `public` are mutually exclusive, but both can be used with `shared`. The APIC defaults to `private` when unset during creation.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `subnet_control` (String) Determines the Subnet's Control State. The `querier_ip` option is used to treat the gateway_ip as an IGMP querier source IP. The `nd_ra` option is used to treat the gateway_ip address as a Neighbor Discovery Router Advertisement Prefix. The `no_gw` option is used to remove default gateway functionality from the gateway address. The APIC defaults to `nd_ra` when unset during creation.
- `subnet_name` (String) The name of the Subnet.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tenant` (String) The name of the Tenant.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
